description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, red-tiled gable roof with a distinctive medieval or Tudor-style timber frame exterior. The walls are white with dark wooden beams arranged in a decorative pattern, and the structure is elevated on wooden posts, creating an open space beneath part of the building. The house includes a stone or brick stairway leading up to the main entrance, which is set back under the overhanging upper story. Windows are small and rectangular, framed by dark wood, and are asymmetrically placed on the facade. The overall design suggests a historic European urban dwelling, likely found in a dense neighborhood or along a cobblestone street.
description: A timber-framed, gable-roofed house with red tiles, white walls, and dark wooden beams, elevated on posts with a stone stairway entry.
description_view_0: Front view shows the overhanging upper story, timber framing, and the main entry area beneath the house.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the steep gable roof, exposed beams, and the open space beneath the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back gable, roof intersection, and the continuation of the timber frame pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the stone stairway, side windows, and the full length of the red-tiled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry beneath overhanging upper story, accessed by stone steps, with a simple wooden door and no porch.
